title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Endowment vs Term Plan
An endowment plan and a term plan are two distinct types of life insurance policies that are available in the market. Both these plans differ in terms of their features, benefits payable, tenure, premium, etc. So, let’s understand the differences between an endowment plan and a term plan.
What is term insurance?
A term insurance policy is a protection-oriented life insurance policy. The objective of the plan is to cover the risk of premature death during the policy tenure. If the insured dies during the policy term, a guaranteed death benefit is paid, which is usually the sum assured that you have chosen.
Term plans have low premiums since they usually cover only the risk of death. As such, you can opt for a high sum assured that takes care of your family’s financial needs in your absence.
What is an Endowment Plan?
An endowment plan is a savings-oriented life insurance policy. The plan aims to create a guaranteed corpus over the policy tenure so that you can meet your financial goals. Endowment plans pay a death benefit if the insured dies during the policy tenure. On the other hand, if the insured survives the tenure and the plan matures, a guaranteed maturity benefit is paid.
Endowment plans can also allow bonus additions allowing you to enhance your savings corpus during the policy tenure. Besides bonuses, many plans also provide guaranteed additions, loyalty additions, and other forms of additions to the policy benefits for attractive returns.
Points of Difference Between Endowment Plan and Term Plan
Given below are the primary points that differentiate between endowment vs term insurance plans.
Premium
The premium for a term insurance policy is very low since the coverage is only for the risk of premature death. Term plans are said to be the cheapest life insurance policies having the lowest premiums.
Endowment plans, on the other hand, have higher premiums since they provide a wider scope of coverage. Since there is a saving element in endowment plans which promises a maturity benefit, the premiums are higher.
Returns
Term insurance plans do not offer additional returns. The sum assured is paid if the insured dies during the policy term. Even if you opt for a return of premium policies, the premiums paid are refunded back on maturity, usually without any additional returns.
Endowment plans, on the other hand, give returns on your investments. If you choose participating endowment plans, you can earn a bonus every year the insurer earns a profit. Moreover, there are guaranteed additions, boosters, loyalty additions, etc., wherein a percentage of the sum assured or premium is added to the plan benefits. These additions form the plan returns and generate a higher corpus on death or maturity.
Protection
Term insurance plans provide unparalleled protection against the risk of premature death. Since the premiums are low, you can opt for higher levels of sum assured for maximum financial protection for your family.
Endowment plans also provide life insurance protection since a guaranteed death benefit is paid if the insured passes away during the policy tenure. However, since the premiums are higher, affording a high sum assured might prove challenging. As such, the scope of protection in endowment plans is limited compared to term insurance plans.
Tax Benefits
The tax benefits under both policies are similar. Premiums paid for both term and endowment plans qualify as a deduction under Section 80C up to Rs.1.5 lakhs, subject limit specified therein.
The death benefit is completely tax-free under both plans. Moreover, under endowment plans and return of premium term plans, the maturity benefit is also tax-free under Section 10(10D), subject to satisfaction of conditions mentioned therein.
Benefits Upon Maturity
Most term insurance plans do not have any maturity benefit. They simply pay the sum assured if the insured dies during the policy tenure. Under the return of premium plans, however, you get a maturity benefit in the form of a refund of the premiums paid.
Endowment plans pay a maturity benefit. This benefit is the sum assured along with any bonus, guaranteed additions or other additions added during the policy tenure.
If the Life Assured Passes Away
Term insurance plans pay the sum assured if the life assured passes away during the policy term. The plan is, then, terminated.
Endowment plans pay the sum assured along with any bonus, guaranteed additions, loyalty additions or any other forms of additions added to the policy before the date of death. The policy is then terminated.
Impact of discontinuation of the premium
Under term insurance plans, if you discontinue the premium payment, you lose everything. There’s usually no concept of a paid-up value or a surrender value under these plans.
Under endowment plans, discontinuation of premiums does not make you lose everything. If you have paid premiums for a minimum number of years, you get a paid-up value. You can even surrender the policy before the completion of its tenure and earn a surrender value.
Liquidity
There is no liquidity under term insurance plans. If you surrender the policy, you lose out on the policy benefits. Moreover, there is no facility for a policy loan.
Under endowment plans, you can get liquidity by availing of policy loans against the surrender value.
How to Choose Between Term Insurance and Endowment Plan?
If you are weighing both plans to figure out which one is better, here are a few factors that would help you make the right choice –
Financial Objective
The first thing to assess is your financial objective. If you want to create a secured financial corpus for your family that sufficiently takes care of their needs, a term insurance plan would be a good choice. It would secure your family’s finances in your absence. On the other hand, if you want to create a savings corpus for your financial goals and are looking for a guaranteed maturity benefit, endowment plans would do the needful.
Current & Future Expenses
Take into account the current and future expenses of the household. Choose a plan whose sum assured would be sufficient to cover these expenses in your absence. A term insurance plan can help in replacing the lost income for the family if the breadwinner dies. The plan takes care of your family’s expenses if you opt for a high sum assured.
Even when buying endowment plans, knowing the current and future expenses help you to pick the right sum assured for the policy so that you can create an optimal corpus for your financial goals.
Affordability
Check which plan would give you affordable coverage and fulfil your goals. A term insurance plan scores in this context as its premiums are low. It allows you to opt for a high sum assured without worrying about high premiums.
Life Goals
The choice of the plan also depends on your life goals. This is where endowment assurance plans can help. These plans help in creating a guaranteed corpus for your goals, complete with additional returns in the form of bonuses and other types of additions. Even term insurance plans can help your family fulfil their goals in your absence as they receive financial assistance from the policy.
Though both endowment and term insurance plans are different from one another, both have their respective benefits. A term insurance plan is relevant for you if you want to leave behind a secured financial corpus for your family’s financial needs in the case of unforeseen contingencies. On the other hand, an endowment plan helps you plan and save for your financial goals with the promise of a guaranteed corpus and also life insurance protection. You can, thus, buy both these policies and take care of your financial responsibilities and obligations.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Do I Get Maturity as well as Death benefits with an Endowment Plan?
You get either a death benefit or a maturity benefit with an endowment plan, not both. In the case of death, the plan pays a death benefit to your nominee. On the other hand, if the plan matures and you survive the tenure, you get a maturity benefit.
How Much Life Cover Should I Choose with Term Insurance?
The choice of the life cover should depend on your financial needs and requirements. To determine the coverage, consider the following factors –
Your present expenses
Current assets and liabilities
Number of dependents
Expected future expenses
Financial goals
Then you can calculate the coverage using different formulae. Insurance companies also offer online term insurance calculators that help you find the suitable sum assured level depending on your financial information. Use the calculator to gain insight into the right coverage amount.
Can the Duration of Life Cover be Changed with Term Insurance?
No, once you buy a term insurance policy, the coverage tenure cannot be changed.
Are there any Riders Available with an Endowment Plan?
Yes, endowment plans offer different types of optional riders so that you can enhance the scope of coverage of the policy. Some of the riders that are available include the following –
Accidental death and disability benefit rider
Critical illness rider
Premium waiver rider
Term rider.
Which is better, term insurance or an endowment plan?
Both policies have their respective pros and cons. You should assess your financial needs and goals and decide which policy would be suitable for your needs. Alternatively, you can invest in both plans to enjoy each of their benefits.
What is the difference between term insurance and endowment policy?
A term insurance policy is a pure protection policy that pays the sum assured if the insured dies during the policy tenure. There’s usually no maturity benefit under term plans.
Endowment plans, on the other hand, have a saving element along with insurance cover. They pay either a death benefit or a maturity benefit helping you secure your finances and also plan for your financial goals.
Term plans are cheaper than endowment plans and do not offer any bonuses or additions. Endowment plans, on the other hand, offer returns in the form of bonuses and other additions.
Is it good to buy endowment insurance plans?
Yes, endowment insurance plans can prove to be a beneficial addition to your portfolio. Here are some reasons why –
They give guaranteed benefits on death or maturity
They help you create a secured corpus for your financial goals
The insurance coverage offered by the plan provides financial security
You can earn additional returns in the form of bonuses, guaranteed additions, etc.
You also get tax benefits.
How can I buy term insurance online?
Yes, you can buy a term insurance plan online and get instant coverage. Most insurance companies allow the online mode of buying the plan, wherein you fill out an online proposal form, upload your documents and pay the premium digitally.
How can I buy an endowment plan online?
Yes, you can. Many insurance providers allow some of their endowment plans through online channels. You can invest in such plans and get instant coverage.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.

full_pdf_text:
Endowment vs Term Plan
An endowment plan and a term plan are two distinct types of life insurance policies that are available in the market. Both these plans differ in terms of their features, benefits payable, tenure, premium, etc. So, let’s understand the differences between an endowment plan and a term plan.
What is term insurance?
A term insurance policy is a protection-oriented life insurance policy. The objective of the plan is to cover the risk of premature death during the policy tenure. If the insured dies during the policy term, a guaranteed* death benefit is paid, which is usually the sum assured that you have chosen.
Term plans have low premiums since they usually cover only the risk of death. As such, you can opt for a high sum assured that takes care of your family’s financial needs in your absence.
What is an Endowment Plan?
An endowment plan is a savings-oriented life insurance policy. The plan aims to create a guaranteed* corpus over the policy tenure so that you can meet your financial goals. Endowment plans pay a death benefit if the insured dies during the policy tenure. On the other hand, if the insured survives the tenure and the plan matures, a guaranteed* maturity benefit is paid.
Endowment plans can also allow bonus additions, if any declared by the insurance company, allowing you to enhance your savings corpus during the policy tenure. Besides bonuses, many plans also provide guaranteed* additions, loyalty additions, and other forms of additions to the policy benefits.
Points of Difference between Endowment Plan and Term Plan
Given below are the primary points that differentiate between endowment vs term insurance plans.
1. Premium
The premium for a term insurance policy is affordable since the coverage is only for the risk of premature death during policy term. Term plans are said to be one of the life insurance policies having the lowest premiums. Endowment plans, on the other hand, have higher premiums since they provide a wider scope of coverage. Since there is a saving element in endowment plans which promises a maturity benefit, the premiums are higher. 2. Returns
Term insurance plans do not offer additional returns. The sum assured is paid if the insured dies during the policy term. Even if you opt for a return of premium policies, the premiums paid are refunded back on maturity, usually without any additional returns. Endowment plans, on the other hand, give returns on your investments. If you choose participating endowment plans, you can earn a bonus if an insurer earns a profit and declares bonus. Moreover, for some plans, there are guaranteed* additions, boosters, loyalty additions, etc., wherein a percentage of the sum assured or premium is added to the plan benefits. These additions helps in generating a higher corpus on death or maturity. 3. Protection
Term insurance plans provide unparalleled protection against the risk of premature death. Since the premiums are affordable, you can opt for higher levels of sum assured for maximum financial protection for your family. Endowment plans also provide life insurance protection since a guaranteed* death benefit is paid if the insured passes away during the policy tenure. However, since the premiums are higher, affording a high sum assured might prove challenging. 4. Tax Benefits
The tax benefits under both policies are similar. Premiums paid for both term and endowment plans qualify as a deduction under Section 80C up to Rs.1.5 lakhs, subject limit specified under Income Tax Act, 1961 (the Act). The death benefit is completely tax-free under both plans. Moreover, under endowment plans and return of premium term plans, the maturity benefit is also tax-free under Section 10(10D), subject to satisfaction of conditions mentioned under the Act. 5. Benefits Upon Maturity
Most term insurance plans do not have any maturity benefit. They simply pay the sum assured if the insured dies during the policy tenure. Under the return of premium plans, however, you get a maturity benefit in the form of a refund of the premiums paid as per policy terms & conditions. Endowment plans pay a maturity benefit. This benefit is the sum assured along with any bonus (if declared), guaranteed* additions or other additions added during the policy tenure in line with the terms of the policy. 6. If the Life Assured Passes Away
Term insurance plans pay the sum assured if the life assured passes away during the policy term. The plan is, then, terminated. Endowment plans pay the sum assured along with bonus, if any, guaranteed* additions, loyalty additions or any other forms of additions added to the policy before the date of death in line with the terms of the policy. The policy is then terminated. 7. Impact of discontinuation of the premium
Under term insurance plans, if you discontinue the premium payment, the policy lapses and no benefits are payable under the policy. There’s usually no concept of a paid-up value or a surrender value under these plans. Under endowment plans, discontinuation of premiums does not make you lose everything. If you have paid premiums for a minimum number of years, you get a paid-up value subject to policy terms & conditions. You can even surrender the policy before the completion of its tenure and earn a surrender value as per policy terms & conditions. 8. Liquidity
There is no liquidity under term insurance plans. If you surrender the policy, you lose out on the policy benefits. Moreover, there is no facility for a policy loan.
Under endowment plans, you can get liquidity by availing of policy loans against the surrender value.
How to Choose Between Term Insurance and Endowment Plan?
If you are weighing both plans to figure out which one is suitable, here are a few factors that would help you make the right choice –
1. Financial Objective
The first thing to assess is your financial objective. If you want to create a secured financial corpus for your family that sufficiently takes care of their needs, a term insurance plan may be preferred. It would secure your family’s finances in your absence. On the other hand, if you want to create a savings corpus for your financial goals and are looking for a guaranteed* maturity benefit, endowment plans would do the needful. 2. Current & Future Expenses
Take into account the current and future expenses of the household. Choose a plan whose sum assured would be sufficient to cover these expenses in your absence. A term insurance plan can help in replacing the lost income for the family if the breadwinner dies. The plan takes care of your family’s expenses if you opt for a high sum assured. Even when buying endowment plans, knowing the current and future expenses help you to pick the right sum assured for the policy so that you can create an optimal corpus for your financial goals. 3. Affordability
Check which plan would give you affordable coverage and fulfil your goals. Premiums for term insurance plans are relatively affordable. It allows you to opt for a high sum assured without worrying about high premiums. 4. Life Goals
The choice of the plan also depends on your life goals. Endowment plans help in creating a guaranteed* corpus for your goals, complete with additional returns in the form of bonuses, if any, and other types of additions. Even term insurance plans can help your family fulfil their goals in your absence as they receive financial assistance from the policy.
Though both endowment and term insurance plans are different from one another, both have their respective benefits. A term insurance plan is relevant for you if you want to leave behind a secured financial corpus for your family’s financial needs in the case of unforeseen contingencies. On the other hand, an endowment plan helps you plan and save for your financial goals with the promise of a guaranteed* corpus and also life insurance protection. You can, thus, consider buying both these policies and take care of your financial responsibilities and obligations.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
1. Do I Get Maturity as well as Death benefits with an Endowment Plan?
You get either a death benefit or a maturity benefit with an endowment plan, not both. In the case of death, the plan pays a death benefit to your nominee. On the other hand, if the plan
matures and you survive the tenure, you get a maturity benefit. 2. How Much Life Cover Should I Choose with Term Insurance?
The choice of the life cover should depend on your financial needs and requirements. To determine the coverage, you may consider the following factors –
o Your present expenses o Current assets and liabilities o Number of dependents o Expected future expenses o Financial goals Then you can calculate the coverage using different formulae. Insurance companies also offer online term insurance calculators that help you find the suitable sum assured level depending on your financial information. Use the calculator to gain insight into the right coverage amount. 3. Can the Duration of Life Cover be changed with Term Insurance?
No, once you buy a term insurance policy, the coverage tenure cannot be changed. 4. Are there any Riders Available with an Endowment Plan?
Yes, endowment plans offer different types of optional riders so that you can enhance the scope of coverage of the policy. The riders can be opted on payment of extra nominal premium. Some of the riders that are available include the following –
o Accidental death and disability benefit rider o Critical illness rider o Premium waiver rider o Term rider.
5. Which is better, term insurance or an endowment plan?
Both policies have their respective pros and cons. You should assess your financial needs and goals and decide which policy would be suitable for your needs. Alternatively, you can invest in both plans to enjoy each of their benefits. 6. What is the difference between term insurance and endowment policy?
A term insurance policy is a pure protection policy that pays the sum assured if the insured dies during the policy tenure. There’s usually no maturity benefit under term plans except in cases where you opt for a term plan with return of premium option, where maturity benefit is limited to the premiums paid. Endowment plans, on the other hand, have a saving element along with insurance cover. They pay either a death benefit or a maturity benefit helping you secure your finances and also plan for your financial goals. Term plans are more affordable and do not offer any bonuses or additions. Endowment plans, on the other hand, offer returns in the form of bonuses (if any) and other additions as per policy terms and conditions. 7. Is it good to buy endowment insurance plans?
Yes, endowment insurance plans can prove to be a beneficial addition to your portfolio. Here are some reasons why –
o They give guaranteed* benefits on death or maturity o They help you create a secured corpus for your financial goals o The insurance coverage offered by the plan provides financial security o You can earn additional returns in the form of bonuses (if any), guaranteed* additions,
etc depending on product terms and conditions. o You also get tax benefits subject to provisions stated under Income Tax Act, 1961.
8. How can I buy term insurance online?
You can buy a term insurance plan online and get instant coverage. Most insurance companies allow the online mode of buying the plan, wherein you fill out an online proposal form, upload your documents and pay the premium digitally. Disclaimer: The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.

changes.word_original: There’s usually no maturity benefit under term plans.
changes.pdf_final: There’s usually no maturity benefit under term plans except in cases where you opt for a term plan with return of premium option, where maturity benefit is limited to the premiums paid.

changes.word_original: Term plans are cheaper than endowment plans and do not offer any bonuses or additions.
changes.pdf_final: Term plans are more affordable and do not offer any bonuses or additions.

changes.word_original: Endowment plans, on the other hand, offer returns in the form of bonuses and other additions.
changes.pdf_final: Endowment plans, on the other hand, offer returns in the form of bonuses (if any) and other additions as per policy terms and conditions. 7.
